title: Prediction: These Will Be Micron's and Sandisk's Stock Prices by the End of 2027
author: Keithen Drury
description: Micron and Sandisk are positioned for significant growth through 2027 due to surging demand for memory chips driven by AI infrastructure expansion. With supply severely constrained and data center capex expected to reach $1 trillion in 2027, analysts project Micron could reach $2,276 per share and Sandisk $3,661 per share by end of fiscal 2027 if trading at 20x earnings, with potential to exceed $2,500 and $4,000 respectively if AI buildout accelerates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong earnings growth expected (EPS rising from $21.18 to $113.81), benefits from severe supply shortage, makes both NAND and DRAM with high demand from AI buildout, stock price projection of $2,276-$2,500+ by end of 2027

Exceptional earnings growth expected (EPS rising from $28.77 to $183.05), NAND memory specialist positioned to benefit from data center expansion, stock price projection of $3,661-$4,000+ by end of 2027, supply constraints support pricing power
Referenced as projecting $1 trillion in combined hyperscaler capex for 2027 and $3-4 trillion in global data center capex by 2030, validating strong demand outlook that benefits memory chip suppliers

title: Nasdaq 100 Rallies, Oil Sinks To $75 On Iran Peace Deal: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks rebounded Thursday with the Nasdaq 100 leading gains on a semiconductor rally fueled by an Intel-Apple chip manufacturing partnership. Oil prices tumbled 2.2% to $75/barrel following a U.S.-Iran peace deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The S&P 500 rose 1.2% while the Russell 2000 fell 0.7% amid mixed market performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 9.8% following announcement of Apple partnership to design and build chips in America, extending its strong 400%+ run since 2025 government investment
Benefited from semiconductor sector rally and positive sentiment from Intel partnership announcement, though modest 0.49% gain reflects broader tech strength
Rose 3.9% in sympathy with Intel's surge and broader semiconductor sector strength
Gained 4.24% as semiconductor peers rallied on Intel-Apple deal and AI-silicon momentum
Climbed 12.1% riding the AI-silicon rally and semiconductor sector strength
Added 11.5% benefiting from AI-silicon rally and semiconductor sector momentum
Rose 11.0% on strength in memory pricing within the semiconductor sector
Plunged 17.4% after cutting fiscal-year revenue-growth guidance due to cautious enterprise spending and slowdown in U.S. federal business
Fell 10.4% on Accenture's negative guidance read-across affecting the entire digital-consulting cohort
Dropped 9.9% due to Accenture's cautious outlook impacting the consulting sector
Lost 9.8% as consulting sector repriced following Accenture's revenue guidance cut
Slid 8.7% on Accenture's negative read-across to the IT services and consulting industry

title: Take Chips Off The Table Before AI Trade Turns, UBS Warns
author: Erica Kollmann
description: UBS warns investors to reduce risk in tech stocks as the AI trade becomes increasingly crowded and overvalued. The bank cautions that semiconductor valuations have become extreme with a binary 'winners vs losers' framework, and flags vulnerabilities in the data-center demand thesis that has supported earnings growth. UBS highlights concerning macro conditions including elevated leverage, weak market breadth, and one-sided sentiment that historically precede sharp corrections.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Semiconductor company caught in the crowded AI trade with extreme valuations. UBS warning applies directly to semis sector, and the article highlights vulnerability in data-center demand that supports earnings.
Semiconductor company exposed to the same AI trade risks and valuation concerns flagged by UBS. Part of the semis cohort with binary winner/loser framework.
Memory chip manufacturer directly impacted by UBS's warning about semiconductor valuations becoming extreme and vulnerable to correction in the crowded AI trade.

title: Why Sandisk Stock Just Jumped
author: Rich Smith
description: President Trump announced a partnership between Apple and Intel for semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S., causing Sandisk stock to surge 10.7%, Intel to jump 9.5%, and Apple to gain nearly 1%. The deal, combined with Intel's collaborations with SpaceX and Tesla on AI chips, is expected to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing. However, analysts note Sandisk's 67x P/E ratio appears steep despite strong growth prospects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock surged 10.7% on news of increased semiconductor manufacturing demand. Memory chips are essential for AI chip inference, positioning the company to benefit from the AI revolution and domestic manufacturing boom. However, valuation at 67x earnings is noted as a concern.

Stock jumped 9.5% following the announcement of the Apple partnership for semiconductor manufacturing. Additional collaborations with SpaceX and Tesla on AI chips further support positive outlook for domestic manufacturing expansion.

Stock gained only 0.5%, showing minimal market reaction despite being a key partner in the Intel manufacturing deal. The modest gain suggests investors view this as a positive but not transformative development for Apple.
Mentioned as collaborating with Intel on AI chip manufacturing at the Terrafab megafactory, positioning the company to benefit from domestic semiconductor production expansion.
Announced collaboration with Intel for AI chip and semiconductor manufacturing, indicating involvement in the domestic manufacturing boom, though stock performance not detailed in main article.

title: Apple Will Raise Prices Due To Soaring Memory Costs: CEO Tim Cook Says 'This Is A 100-Year Flood'
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced the company will raise prices across its product lineup due to skyrocketing memory and storage component costs. Cook described the situation as a "100-year flood," citing a dramatic reallocation of DRAM and NAND supply toward AI infrastructure by major tech companies. Memory chip prices have quadrupled as companies like Google, Microsoft, Meta, and Amazon expand AI capex.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Apple must raise prices due to unsustainable cost pressures, which could impact consumer demand and competitiveness.
As a dominant DRAM producer, Micron benefits from soaring memory prices and increased demand for AI infrastructure, with stock up 900% from 52-week low.
Storage chipmaker benefits significantly from price increases, with stock up 4,785% from its 52-week low of $40.10.
